codeReview.summary: Reviewed Sources/Persistence/{Keys,Settings,PersistenceStore}.swift and Tests/KiteTests/PersistenceTests/PersistenceStoreTests.swift (9 cases). Architecture is clean: a single JSON blob at PersistenceKeys.settingsBlob, JSONEncoder/Decoder, schema-version probe that dispatches before full decode, typed mutators that each call save() synchronously. `@Observable @MainActor` isolates mutations to the main actor; UserDefaults injection via init parameter enables clean test isolation with per-test UUID-keyed suites. Corrupt-blob recovery falls back to defaults and overwrites the garbage so subsequent launches don't re-hit the slow path. Schema v1 is the baseline; the migration hook exists and throws on unknown versions. One design concern about drift between the per-field Keys.swift registry and the aggregate blob, plus the documented naming deviation (KiteSettings vs Settings) are the main items for follow-up.

issues.description: PersistenceKeys declares 9 per-field keys (schemaVersion, pinnedRepos, extraRoots, lastOpenedRepo, lastSelectedBranch, windowFrame, sidebarWidth, detailWidth, autoFetchEnabled) but PersistenceStore only uses `settingsBlob`. The handoff explicitly warns that future features binding via @AppStorage on a per-field key would create drift with the blob. This is real: `@AppStorage("nl.rb2.kite.autoFetchEnabled")` in M5-auto-fetch would write to a different UserDefaults key than `store.settings.autoFetchEnabled`, and the two would silently diverge. Recommend either (a) removing the unused keys and mandating all reads/writes go through PersistenceStore, or (b) adding a deliberate split where those keys are written by PersistenceStore in parallel with the blob. As written, the class-doc promises @AppStorage will Just Work, but it won't. Pick one strategy and document it in AGENTS.md so M5 doesn't walk into the drift.

The type is `KiteSettings` rather than `Settings` to avoid collision with SwiftUI's `Settings` Scene in KiteApp.swift. The handoff documents this. The mission brief (features.json M1-persistence.expectedBehavior) uses 'Settings struct' terminology. Propose adding one sentence to AGENTS.md Conventions → Naming explicitly stating 'Codable persistence root is `KiteSettings`; avoid the bare name `Settings` in source files imported from SwiftUI-using modules.' M2-settings-roots will otherwise re-hit this with Settings tab view names.

`write(_:to:)` silently swallows a JSONEncoder throw with only a comment. This is correct defensively (today KiteSettings is fully Codable), but swallowed errors make regressions invisible. If a future field forgets Codable conformance, the first visible symptom would be 'persistence quietly stops working.' Recommend adding `os.Logger(subsystem: "nl.rb2.kite", category: "persist").error("Failed to encode KiteSettings: \(error.localizedDescription)")` per AGENTS.md observability guidance. Zero cost when it never fires; essential diagnostic when it does.

load() catches `error` but the caught identifier is unused (`do { ... } catch { return ... }`). SwiftLint doesn't flag this but it's lossy — the exact failure (SchemaProbe decode vs KiteSettings decode vs MigrationError) is thrown away before anyone can log it. Same remediation: `Logger.error("Persistence recovery: corrupt blob, resetting to defaults: \(error)")`. Without this, a user reporting 'my settings got reset' has no signal to diagnose with.

setWindowFrame(_ rect: CGRect) saves on every call. Window resize produces many CGRect updates per second. If a M8 feature binds directly to window-resize events, this will cause a save() per frame — UserDefaults writes are cheap but not free. Recommend debouncing (a 250 ms Timer before save()) or limiting this mutator to window-close events. Not a correctness issue, but a perf footgun waiting for M8.

`fatalError` on UserDefaults(suiteName:) returning nil is fine for tests but the suite name `"test-" + UUID().uuidString` is implicitly a valid UserDefaults domain. In practice UserDefaults(suiteName:) returns nil only for reserved names (e.g. the main app's bundle id). Leaving fatalError is OK, but it would be nicer to use Swift Testing's `Issue.record` + `throw` so the test reports a clean failure rather than crashing the runner.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Error enums in this feature (ExtraRootError, MigrationError) are nested inside PersistenceStore rather than at file top-level. M1-git-engine uses top-level GitError. Neither is wrong, but mixing patterns will confuse future workers. Recommend AGENTS.md pick one — I lean top-level (M1-git-engine's style) because they are referenced from tests and callers outside the owning class.

Per-test UserDefaults isolation via `UserDefaults(suiteName: "test-" + UUID().uuidString)` with `removePersistentDomain(forName:)` cleanup is the right pattern. Future feature tests that need shared state (GitQueue, AutoFetchTimer, Window frame) should adopt the same helper — consider extracting `PersistenceTestHelper.makeIsolatedDefaults()` under Tests/Support/ so M5-auto-fetch doesn't re-implement it.

KiteSettings.windowFrame uses a hand-written CGRectValue wrapper, explicitly rejecting CGRect's synthesized Codable. This is defensive against SDK drift and also keeps on-disk JSON human-readable — worth calling out as a pattern for any future on-disk CGSize / CGPoint fields.
